title: CharacterData
description: Schema for a single character (base or support) in the Remaster Engine. v1.2.0: support_slots refactored to semantic scene_progression (DEC-003a); innate description updated to cover enemies (DEC-005a).

id.description: Lowercase snake_case identifier. Used as filename and lookup key.

element.description: Color element. Per §3.4 + DEC-006/008, 7 elements are locked: 6 color elements + 'neutral' for basic attacks (weak/medium/heavy), physical attacks, and Chrono Cross specials.

level.description: Current level. Per §3.8 locked level-based progression.

is_base.description: True if this is one of the 6 main bases (Serge, Kidd, Nikki, Glenn, Herle, Norris). False for support characters.

innate.description: Per DEC-005/005a: dual-purpose field. For PLAYABLE characters, this is always 'none' (innates come from equipment). For ENEMIES, this describes their behavior pattern (steal/performance/combat/dark/healer). Same field, different meaning per character type. Future EnemyData schema will formalize the enemy-specific behavior enums; for now, character.schema.json accepts both.

stats.description: Base stats for the character. Per DEC-004, stats live on the character. Runtime scaling (per level) handled by StatResolver autoload.

spd.description: Base speed (turn order). Higher = acts first.

basic_attack.description: Display name of the basic attack line. Per §3.5, this is the same as tier_1_tech (the basic attack line's tier-1 form). Always present, always relevant.

tier_1_tech.description: Display name of the tier 1 tech (per §3.7 locked per-base techs). For bases, this matches basic_attack. For supports, this is the tech unlocked at the recruitment scene.

tier_8_tech.description: Display name of the tier 8 ultimate. Per §3.7 locked per-base techs. For supports, this is the tech unlocked at the final scene (often a piece of equipment that provides the tech).

support_id.description: Lowercase snake_case identifier of the support character.

scene_progression.description: Per DEC-003a: 3 semantic scene slots, each representing a story beat that unlocks a tech.

recruitment.description: First tech unlock: at the canon RECRUITMENT scene. Tends to be tier 2 (early recruits) or tier 3 (late recruits).

level_required.description: Optional: base's level must be at least this to use the tech.

story.description: Second tech unlock: at the canon STORYLINE scene (the support's main character moment). Tends to be tier 4 (early recruits) or tier 5 (late recruits).

final.description: Third tech unlock: at the canon FINAL event (often a piece of equipment that grants the tech, or the support's final moment in the story). Tends to be tier 6 (early recruits) or tier 7 (late recruits).

support_slots.description: List of support characters attached to this base. Per DEC-003a, each support has 3 semantic scene_progression slots (recruitment/story/final) representing the story beats that unlock the support's 3 techs. CharacterData loader flattens this into a list of (support_id, scene_name, tech_id, tier) tuples for downstream consumers.

sprite.description: Path to the character's field sprite. Empty string = placeholder rectangle per §15.3 PoC scope.

portrait.description: Path to the character's portrait. Empty string = placeholder.
